prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier orbital) gap of furan, in electron-volts (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted, run in PySCF
Geometry provenance: fixed coordinates read from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point electronic structure calculation → DFT//input-XYZ)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit frontend invoking PySCF (version as in engine build; not reported in JSON)
SCF convergence & caveats: SCF converged (9 cycles); no engine warnings reported.
Live log path: chemkit streamed a frontier_orbitals_[timestamp].out file to the working directory during the run (path was displayed at launch; retain for full SCF/DFT transcript and reproducibility).

Result (from chemkit frontier-orbitals skill):
• HOMO energy  = −6.3597638 eV
• LUMO energy  = −0.1024036 eV
⇒ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap; not an excitation energy)

No warnings were reported in the tool output.
Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true (all automated checks passed).
